global:
  scrape_interval: 15s
  evaluation_interval: 15s
  external_labels:
    cluster: mms-production
    environment: production

alerting:
  alertmanagers:
    - static_configs:
        - targets:
            - alertmanager:9093

rule_files:
  - /etc/prometheus/alerts.yml

scrape_configs:
  - job_name: prometheus
    static_configs:
      - targets: ['localhost:9090']

  - job_name: mms-backend
    kubernetes_sd_configs:
      - role: pod
        namespaces:
          names:
            - mms
    relabel_configs:
      - source_labels: [__meta_kubernetes_pod_label_app_kubernetes_io_component]
        regex: backend
        action: keep
      - source_labels: [__meta_kubernetes_pod_annotation_prometheus_io_scrape]
        regex: "true"
        action: keep
      - source_labels: [__meta_kubernetes_pod_annotation_prometheus_io_path]
        action: replace
        target_label: __metrics_path__
        regex: (.+)
      - source_labels: [__address__, __meta_kubernetes_pod_annotation_prometheus_io_port]
        action: replace
        regex: ([^:]+)(?::\d+)?;(\d+)
        replacement: $1:$2
        target_label: __address__
      - source_labels: [__meta_kubernetes_namespace]
        action: replace
        target_label: namespace
      - source_labels: [__meta_kubernetes_pod_name]
        action: replace
        target_label: pod

  - job_name: mms-workers
    kubernetes_sd_configs:
      - role: pod
        namespaces:
          names:
            - mms
    relabel_configs:
      - source_labels: [__meta_kubernetes_pod_label_app_kubernetes_io_component]
        regex: (worker|optimizer|file-watcher)
        action: keep
      - source_labels: [__meta_kubernetes_pod_annotation_prometheus_io_scrape]
        regex: "true"
        action: keep
      - source_labels: [__meta_kubernetes_pod_annotation_prometheus_io_path]
        action: replace
        target_label: __metrics_path__
        regex: (.+)
      - source_labels: [__address__, __meta_kubernetes_pod_annotation_prometheus_io_port]
        action: replace
        regex: ([^:]+)(?::\d+)?;(\d+)
        replacement: $1:$2
        target_label: __address__
      - source_labels: [__meta_kubernetes_namespace]
        action: replace
        target_label: namespace
      - source_labels: [__meta_kubernetes_pod_name]
        action: replace
        target_label: pod
      - source_labels: [__meta_kubernetes_pod_label_app_kubernetes_io_component]
        action: replace
        target_label: component

  - job_name: mms-redis
    static_configs:
      - targets:
          - redis:6379

  - job_name: mms-postgres
    static_configs:
      - targets:
          - postgres-exporter:9187

file.content:
# MMS Development Docker Compose
#
# Starts all MMS services for local development.
#
# Usage:
#   docker compose up -d                   # Start all services
#   docker compose up -d postgres redis    # Start data services only
#   docker compose logs -f backend         # View backend logs
#   docker compose down -v                 # Stop and remove volumes
#
# Environment:
#   Copy .env.example to .env and configure as needed
#
# Services:
#   - postgres: PostgreSQL 16 + TimescaleDB
#   - redis: Redis 7 for queues and caching
#   - backend: MMS API server (Bun + Hono)
#   - worker: MMS background workers (BullMQ)
#   - frontend: React development server (Vite)
#   - file-watcher: Mileage CSV file watcher
#   - optimizer: C++ OR-Tools optimizer binary
#
# @see specs/008-operations.md

name: mms-dev

services:
  # =============================================================================
  # Data Layer
  # =============================================================================

  postgres:
    image: timescale/timescaledb:2.14.2-pg16
    container_name: mms-postgres
    restart: unless-stopped
    ports:
      - "${POSTGRES_PORT:-5432}:5432"
    environment:
      POSTGRES_DB: ${POSTGRES_DB:-mms}
      POSTGRES_USER: ${POSTGRES_USER:-mms}
      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: ${POSTGRES_PASSWORD:-mms_dev_password}
    volumes:
      - postgres_data:/var/lib/postgresql/data
      - ./drizzle/manual:/docker-entrypoint-initdb.d:ro
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "pg_isready -U ${POSTGRES_USER:-mms} -d ${POSTGRES_DB:-mms}"]
      interval: 10s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 5
      start_period: 10s
    networks:
      - mms-network

  redis:
    image: redis:7-alpine
    container_name: mms-redis
    restart: unless-stopped
    ports:
      - "${REDIS_PORT:-6379}:6379"
    command: >
      redis-server
      --appendonly yes
      --maxmemory 256mb
      --maxmemory-policy allkeys-lru
    volumes:
      - redis_data:/data
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "redis-cli", "ping"]
      interval: 10s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 5
      start_period: 5s
    networks:
      - mms-network

  # =============================================================================
  # Application Layer
  # =============================================================================

  backend:
    build:
      context: .
      dockerfile: docker/Dockerfile.backend
    container_name: mms-backend
    restart: unless-stopped
    ports:
      - "${API_PORT:-3000}:3000"
    environment:
      NODE_ENV: development
      PORT: 3000
      HOST: 0.0.0.0
      DATABASE_URL: postgres://${POSTGRES_USER:-mms}:${POSTGRES_PASSWORD:-mms_dev_password}@postgres:5432/${POSTGRES_DB:-mms}
      REDIS_URL: redis://redis:6379
      JWT_SECRET: ${JWT_SECRET:-dev_jwt_secret_change_in_production}
      LOG_LEVEL: ${LOG_LEVEL:-debug}
      OTEL_ENABLED: ${OTEL_ENABLED:-false}
    depends_on:
      postgres:
        condition: service_healthy
      redis:
        condition: service_healthy
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "wget", "-q", "--spider", "http://localhost:3000/health"]
      interval: 30s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 3
      start_period: 10s
    networks:
      - mms-network
    develop:
      watch:
        - action: sync+restart
          path: ./src
          target: /app/src

  worker:
    build:
      context: .
      dockerfile: docker/Dockerfile.worker
    container_name: mms-worker
    restart: unless-stopped
    environment:
      NODE_ENV: development
      DATABASE_URL: postgres://${POSTGRES_USER:-mms}:${POSTGRES_PASSWORD:-mms_dev_password}@postgres:5432/${POSTGRES_DB:-mms}
      REDIS_URL: redis://redis:6379
      REDIS_HOST: redis
      REDIS_PORT: 6379
      OPTIMIZER_BINARY_PATH: /opt/optimizer/mms-optimizer
      LOG_LEVEL: ${LOG_LEVEL:-debug}
      WORKERS: ${WORKERS:-}
    depends_on:
      postgres:
        condition: service_healthy
      redis:
        condition: service_healthy
      optimizer:
        condition: service_started
    volumes:
      - optimizer_binary:/opt/optimizer:ro
    networks:
      - mms-network
    develop:
      watch:
        - action: sync+restart
          path: ./src
          target: /app/src

  frontend:
    build:
      context: .
      dockerfile: docker/Dockerfile.frontend
      target: development
    container_name: mms-frontend
    restart: unless-stopped
    ports:
      - "${FRONTEND_PORT:-5173}:5173"
    environment:
      VITE_API_URL: http://localhost:${API_PORT:-3000}
    volumes:
      - ./src:/app/src:ro
      - ./public:/app/public:ro
    networks:
      - mms-network

  file-watcher:
    build:
      context: .
      dockerfile: docker/Dockerfile.file-watcher
    container_name: mms-file-watcher
    restart: unless-stopped
    environment:
      NODE_ENV: development
      DATABASE_URL: postgres://${POSTGRES_USER:-mms}:${POSTGRES_PASSWORD:-mms_dev_password}@postgres:5432/${POSTGRES_DB:-mms}
      REDIS_URL: redis://redis:6379
      WATCH_PATH: /data/inbox
      QUARANTINE_PATH: /data/quarantine
      LOG_LEVEL: ${LOG_LEVEL:-debug}
    depends_on:
      postgres:
        condition: service_healthy
      redis:
        condition: service_healthy
    volumes:
      - file_inbox:/data/inbox
      - file_quarantine:/data/quarantine
    networks:
      - mms-network

  # =============================================================================
  # Optimizer Sidecar (C++ Binary)
  # =============================================================================

  optimizer:
    build:
      context: ./mms-optimizer
      dockerfile: Dockerfile
    container_name: mms-optimizer
    # The optimizer binary is spawned by the worker process
    # This container just makes the binary available
    entrypoint: ["/bin/sh", "-c"]
    command: ["cp /app/mms-optimizer /opt/optimizer/ && tail -f /dev/null"]
    volumes:
      - optimizer_binary:/opt/optimizer
    networks:
      - mms-network

  # =============================================================================
  # Development Utilities
  # =============================================================================

  # Redis Commander - Web UI for Redis
  redis-commander:
    image: rediscommander/redis-commander:latest
    container_name: mms-redis-commander
    restart: unless-stopped
    ports:
      - "${REDIS_COMMANDER_PORT:-8081}:8081"
    environment:
      REDIS_HOSTS: local:redis:6379
    depends_on:
      - redis
    networks:
      - mms-network
    profiles:
      - tools

  # pgAdmin - Web UI for PostgreSQL
  pgadmin:
    image: dpage/pgadmin4:latest
    container_name: mms-pgadmin
    restart: unless-stopped
    ports:
      - "${PGADMIN_PORT:-8082}:80"
    environment:
      PGADMIN_DEFAULT_EMAIL: ${PGADMIN_EMAIL:-admin@mms.local}
      PGADMIN_DEFAULT_PASSWORD: ${PGADMIN_PASSWORD:-admin}
      PGADMIN_CONFIG_SERVER_MODE: "False"
    volumes:
      - pgadmin_data:/var/lib/pgadmin
    depends_on:
      - postgres
    networks:
      - mms-network
    profiles:
      - tools

# =============================================================================
# Networks
# =============================================================================

networks:
  mms-network:
    driver: bridge

# =============================================================================
# Volumes
# =============================================================================

volumes:
  postgres_data:
    driver: local
  redis_data:
    driver: local
  optimizer_binary:
    driver: local
  file_inbox:
    driver: local
  file_quarantine:
    driver: local
  pgadmin_data:
    driver: local

file.content:
# MMS Monitoring Stack

Observability configuration for the Metro Fleet Mileage Management System.

## Directory Structure

```
monitoring/
├── grafana/
│   └── dashboards/          # Grafana dashboard JSON files
│       ├── fleet-overview.json
│       ├── pm-schedule.json
│       ├── optimizer-performance.json
│       ├── api-health.json
│       ├── worker-queue.json
│       └── drift-analysis.json
├── prometheus/
│   ├── prometheus.yml       # Prometheus scrape configuration
│   └── alerts.yml           # Alerting rules
├── alertmanager/
│   └── alertmanager.yml     # Alert routing and receivers
└── README.md
```

## Dashboards

| Dashboard | UID | Description |
|-----------|-----|-------------|
| Fleet Overview | `mms-fleet-overview` | Train status, fitness, availability metrics |
| PM Schedule | `mms-pm-schedule` | PM compliance, scheduling, depot utilization |
| Optimizer Performance | `mms-optimizer` | CP-SAT solver metrics, job tracking |
| API Health | `mms-api-health` | Request rates, latency, error tracking |
| Worker Queue | `mms-worker-queue` | BullMQ job processing metrics |
| Drift Analysis | `mms-drift` | Mileage and schedule drift monitoring |

## Alerts

### Fleet Alerts
- `FleetAvailabilityLow` - Availability below 85%
- `FleetAvailabilityCritical` - Availability below 70%
- `TooManyGroundedTrains` - More than 10 grounded
- `MileageVarianceHigh` - Variance exceeds 10,000km

### PM Alerts
- `PMComplianceLow` - Compliance below 95%
- `PMOverdueHigh` - More than 5 overdue
- `DepotOverloaded` - Utilization above 95%

### Optimizer Alerts
- `OptimizerFailureRateHigh` - Failure rate above 10%
- `OptimizerQueueBacklog` - Queue depth above 20
- `OptimizerSlowPerformance` - p95 above 5 minutes

### API Alerts
- `APIHighErrorRate` - Error rate above 5%
- `APIHighLatency` - p95 above 1 second
- `APIDown` - Backend not responding

### Worker Alerts
- `WorkerQueueBacklog` - Queue depth above 100
- `WorkerFailureRateHigh` - Failure rate above 5%
- `WorkersDown` - No active workers

### Drift Alerts
- `DriftThresholdExceeded` - Many trains requiring replan
- `CriticalDriftDetected` - Critical (RED) drift severity

## Deployment

### Kubernetes

1. Create ConfigMaps from config files:

```bash
kubectl create configmap prometheus-config \
  --from-file=prometheus.yml=monitoring/prometheus/prometheus.yml \
  --from-file=alerts.yml=monitoring/prometheus/alerts.yml \
  -n monitoring

kubectl create configmap alertmanager-config \
  --from-file=alertmanager.yml=monitoring/alertmanager/alertmanager.yml \
  -n monitoring

kubectl create configmap grafana-dashboards \
  --from-file=monitoring/grafana/dashboards/ \
  -n monitoring
```

2. Deploy monitoring stack via Helm or manifests

### Docker Compose

Add to your docker-compose.yml:

```yaml
services:
  prometheus:
    image: prom/prometheus:v2.48.0
    volumes:
      - ./monitoring/prometheus:/etc/prometheus
    command:
      - '--config.file=/etc/prometheus/prometheus.yml'
      - '--web.enable-lifecycle'
    ports:
      - 9090:9090

  alertmanager:
    image: prom/alertmanager:v0.26.0
    volumes:
      - ./monitoring/alertmanager:/etc/alertmanager
    command:
      - '--config.file=/etc/alertmanager/alertmanager.yml'
    ports:
      - 9093:9093

  grafana:
    image: grafana/grafana:10.2.0
    volumes:
      - ./monitoring/grafana/dashboards:/etc/grafana/provisioning/dashboards
    environment:
      - GF_SECURITY_ADMIN_PASSWORD=admin
    ports:
      - 3001:3000
```

## Metrics Reference

### Fleet Metrics
- `mms_fleet_total_trains` - Total train count
- `mms_fleet_availability_ratio` - Fleet availability (0-1)
- `mms_fleet_grounded_count` - Grounded trains
- `mms_fleet_restricted_count` - Restricted trains
- `mms_fleet_trains_by_fitness{fitness}` - Trains by fitness status
- `mms_fleet_trains_by_depot{depot}` - Trains by depot
- `mms_train_mileage_variance_km{train_id}` - Mileage variance per train

### PM Metrics
- `mms_pm_compliance_rate` - Overall compliance (0-1)
- `mms_pm_overdue_count` - Overdue PM count
- `mms_pm_scheduled_next_7_days` - PMs in next 7 days
- `mms_pm_in_progress_count` - Active PM count
- `mms_pm_scheduled_by_type{pm_type}` - PMs by type (C1-C4, Overhaul)
- `mms_depot_utilization_rate{depot}` - Depot utilization (0-1)

### Optimizer Metrics
- `mms_optimizer_jobs_total{status}` - Job count by status
- `mms_optimizer_duration_seconds_bucket` - Job duration histogram
- `mms_optimizer_active_jobs` - Currently running jobs
- `mms_optimizer_queue_depth` - Waiting jobs
- `mms_optimizer_memory_usage_bytes` - Memory consumption
- `mms_optimizer_cpu_usage_ratio` - CPU utilization

### API Metrics
- `mms_http_requests_total{method,status,path}` - Request count
- `mms_http_request_duration_seconds_bucket` - Latency histogram
- `mms_websocket_connections_active` - Active WS connections

### Queue Metrics
- `mms_queue_waiting_jobs{queue}` - Waiting jobs per queue
- `mms_queue_active_jobs{queue}` - Active jobs per queue
- `mms_queue_completed_jobs_total{queue}` - Completed count
- `mms_queue_failed_jobs_total{queue}` - Failed count
- `mms_queue_job_duration_seconds_bucket{queue}` - Duration histogram
- `mms_queue_workers_count` - Active worker count

### Drift Metrics
- `mms_drift_trains_requiring_replan` - Trains needing replan
- `mms_train_mileage_drift_km{train_id}` - Drift per train
- `mms_drift_trains_by_severity{severity}` - Count by severity (GREEN/YELLOW/RED)
- `mms_drift_overall_severity` - System-wide severity level

groups:
  - name: mms-fleet
    rules:
      - alert: FleetAvailabilityLow
        expr: mms_fleet_availability_ratio < 0.85
        for: 5m
        labels:
          severity: warning
          team: operations
        annotations:
          summary: "Fleet availability below 85%"
          description: "Fleet availability is {{ $value | humanizePercentage }} which is below the 85% threshold."
          runbook_url: "https://wiki.example.com/runbooks/mms/fleet-availability"

      - alert: FleetAvailabilityCritical
        expr: mms_fleet_availability_ratio < 0.70
        for: 5m
        labels:
          severity: critical
          team: operations
        annotations:
          summary: "Fleet availability critically low"
          description: "Fleet availability is {{ $value | humanizePercentage }} which is critically below 70% threshold."
          runbook_url: "https://wiki.example.com/runbooks/mms/fleet-availability-critical"

      - alert: TooManyGroundedTrains
        expr: mms_fleet_grounded_count > 10
        for: 10m
        labels:
          severity: warning
          team: maintenance
        annotations:
          summary: "Too many grounded trains"
          description: "{{ $value }} trains are currently grounded (threshold: 10)."
          runbook_url: "https://wiki.example.com/runbooks/mms/grounded-trains"

      - alert: MileageVarianceHigh
        expr: abs(mms_train_mileage_variance_km) > 10000
        for: 30m
        labels:
          severity: warning
          team: planning
        annotations:
          summary: "High mileage variance detected"
          description: "Train {{ $labels.train_id }} has {{ $value }}km mileage variance."
          runbook_url: "https://wiki.example.com/runbooks/mms/mileage-variance"

  - name: mms-pm
    rules:
      - alert: PMComplianceLow
        expr: mms_pm_compliance_rate < 0.95
        for: 15m
        labels:
          severity: warning
          team: maintenance
        annotations:
          summary: "PM compliance rate below 95%"
          description: "PM compliance rate is {{ $value | humanizePercentage }}."
          runbook_url: "https://wiki.example.com/runbooks/mms/pm-compliance"

      - alert: PMOverdueHigh
        expr: mms_pm_overdue_count > 5
        for: 5m
        labels:
          severity: warning
          team: maintenance
        annotations:
          summary: "Multiple PMs overdue"
          description: "{{ $value }} preventive maintenance activities are overdue."
          runbook_url: "https://wiki.example.com/runbooks/mms/pm-overdue"

      - alert: DepotOverloaded
        expr: mms_depot_utilization_rate > 0.95
        for: 30m
        labels:
          severity: warning
          team: maintenance
        annotations:
          summary: "Depot capacity overloaded"
          description: "Depot {{ $labels.depot }} utilization is at {{ $value | humanizePercentage }}."
          runbook_url: "https://wiki.example.com/runbooks/mms/depot-capacity"

  - name: mms-optimizer
    rules:
      - alert: OptimizerFailureRateHigh
        expr: |
          sum(rate(mms_optimizer_jobs_total{status=~"ERROR|TIMEOUT"}[1h]))
          /
          sum(rate(mms_optimizer_jobs_total[1h])) > 0.1
        for: 15m
        labels:
          severity: warning
          team: engineering
        annotations:
          summary: "Optimizer failure rate above 10%"
          description: "Optimizer failure rate is {{ $value | humanizePercentage }} in the last hour."
          runbook_url: "https://wiki.example.com/runbooks/mms/optimizer-failures"

      - alert: OptimizerQueueBacklog
        expr: mms_optimizer_queue_depth > 20
        for: 10m
        labels:
          severity: warning
          team: engineering
        annotations:
          summary: "Optimizer queue backlog"
          description: "{{ $value }} jobs waiting in optimizer queue."
          runbook_url: "https://wiki.example.com/runbooks/mms/optimizer-backlog"

      - alert: OptimizerSlowPerformance
        expr: histogram_quantile(0.95, rate(mms_optimizer_duration_seconds_bucket[15m])) > 300
        for: 30m
        labels:
          severity: warning
          team: engineering
        annotations:
          summary: "Optimizer p95 latency above 5 minutes"
          description: "Optimizer p95 duration is {{ $value | humanizeDuration }}."
          runbook_url: "https://wiki.example.com/runbooks/mms/optimizer-slow"

  - name: mms-api
    rules:
      - alert: APIHighErrorRate
        expr: |
          sum(rate(mms_http_requests_total{status=~"5.."}[5m]))
          /
          sum(rate(mms_http_requests_total[5m])) > 0.05
        for: 5m
        labels:
          severity: warning
          team: engineering
        annotations:
          summary: "API error rate above 5%"
          description: "API error rate is {{ $value | humanizePercentage }}."
          runbook_url: "https://wiki.example.com/runbooks/mms/api-errors"

      - alert: APIHighLatency
        expr: histogram_quantile(0.95, rate(mms_http_request_duration_seconds_bucket[5m])) > 1
        for: 10m
        labels:
          severity: warning
          team: engineering
        annotations:
          summary: "API p95 latency above 1 second"
          description: "API p95 latency is {{ $value | humanizeDuration }}."
          runbook_url: "https://wiki.example.com/runbooks/mms/api-latency"

      - alert: APIDown
        expr: up{job="mms-backend"} == 0
        for: 1m
        labels:
          severity: critical
          team: engineering
        annotations:
          summary: "MMS API is down"
          description: "MMS API instance {{ $labels.instance }} is not responding."
          runbook_url: "https://wiki.example.com/runbooks/mms/api-down"

  - name: mms-workers
    rules:
      - alert: WorkerQueueBacklog
        expr: sum(mms_queue_waiting_jobs) > 100
        for: 15m
        labels:
          severity: warning
          team: engineering
        annotations:
          summary: "Worker queue backlog"
          description: "{{ $value }} jobs waiting across all worker queues."
          runbook_url: "https://wiki.example.com/runbooks/mms/worker-backlog"

      - alert: WorkerFailureRateHigh
        expr: |
          sum(rate(mms_queue_failed_jobs_total[1h]))
          /
          sum(rate(mms_queue_completed_jobs_total[1h])) > 0.05
        for: 15m
        labels:
          severity: warning
          team: engineering
        annotations:
          summary: "Worker failure rate above 5%"
          description: "Worker failure rate is {{ $value | humanizePercentage }}."
          runbook_url: "https://wiki.example.com/runbooks/mms/worker-failures"

      - alert: WorkersDown
        expr: mms_queue_workers_count == 0
        for: 5m
        labels:
          severity: critical
          team: engineering
        annotations:
          summary: "No active workers"
          description: "No worker processes are running."
          runbook_url: "https://wiki.example.com/runbooks/mms/workers-down"

  - name: mms-drift
    rules:
      - alert: DriftThresholdExceeded
        expr: mms_drift_trains_requiring_replan > 10
        for: 30m
        labels:
          severity: warning
          team: planning
        annotations:
          summary: "Many trains requiring replan"
          description: "{{ $value }} trains have exceeded drift thresholds and require replanning."
          runbook_url: "https://wiki.example.com/runbooks/mms/drift-threshold"

      - alert: CriticalDriftDetected
        expr: mms_drift_trains_by_severity{severity="RED"} > 5
        for: 15m
        labels:
          severity: critical
          team: planning
        annotations:
          summary: "Critical drift detected"
          description: "{{ $value }} trains have critical (RED) drift severity."
          runbook_url: "https://wiki.example.com/runbooks/mms/critical-drift"

  - name: mms-infrastructure
    rules:
      - alert: DatabaseConnectionPoolExhausted
        expr: mms_db_pool_active_connections / mms_db_pool_max_connections > 0.9
        for: 5m
        labels:
          severity: warning
          team: engineering
        annotations:
          summary: "Database connection pool near exhaustion"
          description: "Database connection pool is at {{ $value | humanizePercentage }} capacity."
          runbook_url: "https://wiki.example.com/runbooks/mms/db-pool"

      - alert: RedisConnectionError
        expr: mms_redis_connection_errors_total > 0
        for: 5m
        labels:
          severity: warning
          team: engineering
        annotations:
          summary: "Redis connection errors"
          description: "Redis connection errors detected."
          runbook_url: "https://wiki.example.com/runbooks/mms/redis-errors"

      - alert: HighMemoryUsage
        expr: process_resident_memory_bytes / 1024 / 1024 / 1024 > 3
        for: 15m
        labels:
          severity: warning
          team: engineering
        annotations:
          summary: "High memory usage"
          description: "Process {{ $labels.job }} using {{ $value | humanize }}GB memory."
          runbook_url: "https://wiki.example.com/runbooks/mms/memory-usage"

1→/**
     2→ * MMS Prometheus Metrics
     3→ *
     4→ * Application metrics for monitoring and alerting.
     5→ * Exposed via /metrics endpoint for Prometheus scraping.
     6→ *
     7→ * Metric types:
     8→ * - Counter: Monotonically increasing values (requests, errors)
     9→ * - Gauge: Point-in-time values (active jobs, queue depth)
    10→ * - Histogram: Distribution of values (request duration, solve time)
    11→ *
    12→ * @see specs/004-backend-specification.md Section 4.14
    13→ */
    14→

   329→
   330→/**
   331→ * HTTP middleware helper - measure request duration
   332→ */
   333→export function measureHttpRequest(
   334→  method: string,
   335→  path: string,
   336→  status: number,
   337→  durationMs: number
   338→): void {
   339→  const labels = { method, path, status: String(status) };
   340→  httpRequestsTotal.inc(labels);
   341→  httpRequestDuration.observe(labels, durationMs / 1000);
   342→}
   343→
   344→/**
   345→ * Queue job helper - record job completion
   346→ */
   347→export function recordQueueJob(
   348→  queue: string,
   349→  jobName: string,
   350→  status: 'completed' | 'failed',
   351→  durationMs: number
   352→): void {
   353→  queueJobsTotal.inc({ queue, status });
   354→  queueJobDuration.observe({ queue, job_name: jobName }, durationMs / 1000);
   355→}
   356→
   357→/**
   358→ * Optimizer helper - record solve completion
   359→ */
   360→export function recordOptimizerRun(
   361→  solverType: 'cpsat' | 'fallback',
   362→  status: 'optimal' | 'feasible' | 'infeasible' | 'timeout' | 'error',
   363→  durationMs: number,
   364→  objective?: number
   365→): void {
   366→  optimizerRunsTotal.inc({ solver_type: solverType, status });
   367→  optimizerSolveSeconds.observe({ solver_type: solverType, status }, durationMs / 1000);
   368→  if (objective !== undefined) {
   369→    optimizerObjectiveValue.set({ solver_type: solverType }, objective);
   370→  }
   371→}

   647→    help: 'Number of shadow records in error state',
   648→    registers: [registry],
   649→  }),
   650→};
   651→
   652→/**
   653→ * Record Maximo sync operation
   654→ */
   655→export function recordMaximoSync(
   656→  operation: 'push' | 'pull' | 'full' | 'polling',
   657→  status: 'success' | 'failure' | 'partial',
   658→  durationMs: number,
   659→  recordsProcessed: number,
   660→  recordsUpdated: number
   661→): void {
   662→  maximoMetrics.syncRequestsTotal.inc({ operation, status });
   663→  maximoMetrics.syncDuration.observe({ operation }, durationMs / 1000);
   664→  maximoMetrics.syncRecordsProcessed.inc({ operation, result: 'processed' }, recordsProcessed);
   665→  maximoMetrics.syncRecordsProcessed.inc({ operation, result: 'updated' }, recordsUpdated);
   666→}
   667→
   668→/**
   669→ * Record webhook received
   670→ */
   671→export function recordMaximoWebhook(
   672→  eventType: string,
   673→  status: 'success' | 'failure',
   674→  durationMs: number,
   675→  depotId?: string
   676→): void {
   677→  maximoMetrics.webhooksReceived.inc({ event_type: eventType, status });
   678→  maximoMetrics.webhookProcessingDuration.observe({ event_type: eventType }, durationMs / 1000);
   679→  if (status === 'success') {
   680→    maximoMetrics.webhookLastReceivedAt.set({ depot_id: depotId ?? 'global' }, Date.now() / 1000);
   681→  }
   682→}
   683→
   684→/**
   685→ * Record polling fallback activation
   686→ */
   687→export function recordPollingFallbackActivation(reason: 'webhook_timeout' | 'manual' | 'circuit_breaker'): void {
   688→  maximoMetrics.pollingTriggered.inc({ trigger_reason: reason });
   689→}
   690→
   691→/**
   692→ * Update sync mode metric
   693→ */
   694→export function updateSyncModeMetric(depotId: string, mode: 'WEBHOOK' | 'POLLING' | 'HYBRID'): void {
   695→  const modeValue = mode === 'WEBHOOK' ? 0 : mode === 'POLLING' ? 1 : 2;
   696→  maximoMetrics.syncMode.set({ depot_id: depotId }, modeValue);
   697→  maximoMetrics.pollingFallbackActive.set({ depot_id: depotId }, mode !== 'WEBHOOK' ? 1 : 0);
   698→}
   699→
   700→// =============================================================================
   701→// Rate Limiting Metrics
   702→// =============================================================================
   703→
   704→/**
   705→ * Rate limiting metrics for monitoring and alerting
   706→ *
   707→ * @see specs/005-api.md - Rate Limiting: 100 requests/minute per user
   708→ */
   709→export const rateLimitMetrics = {
   710→  /**
   711→   * Total rate limit checks performed
   712→   */
   713→  rateLimitChecks: new Counter({
   714→    name: 'mms_rate_limit_checks_total',
   715→    help: 'Total number of rate limit checks performed',
   716→    labelNames: ['identifier_type', 'result'] as const,
   717→    registers: [registry],
   718→  }),
   719→
   720→  /**
   721→   * Rate limit exceeded events
   722→   */
   723→  rateLimitExceeded: new Counter({
   724→    name: 'mms_rate_limit_exceeded_total',
   725→    help: 'Total number of requests rejected due to rate limiting',
   726→    labelNames: ['identifier_type'] as const,
   727→    registers: [registry],
   728→  }),
   729→
   730→  /**
   731→   * Current rate limit window usage per identifier
   732→   */
   733→  rateLimitUsage: new Gauge({
   734→    name: 'mms_rate_limit_usage',
   735→    help: 'Current rate limit usage percentage',
   736→    labelNames: ['identifier_type'] as const,
   737→    registers: [registry],
   738→  }),
   739→};
   740→
   741→/**
   742→ * Record rate limit check result
   743→ */
   744→export function recordRateLimitCheck(
   745→  identifierType: 'user' | 'ip',
   746→  result: 'allowed' | 'exceeded'
   747→): void {
   748→  rateLimitMetrics.rateLimitChecks.inc({ identifier_type: identifierType, result });
   749→  if (result === 'exceeded') {
   750→    rateLimitMetrics.rateLimitExceeded.inc({ identifier_type: identifierType });
   751→  }
   752→}

# MMS Kubernetes Manifests

Kubernetes deployment configuration for the Metro Fleet Mileage Management System.

## Directory Structure

```
k8s/
├── base/                    # Base configuration
│   ├── namespace.yaml       # Namespace, ResourceQuota, LimitRange
│   ├── configmap.yaml       # Environment configuration
│   └── secrets.yaml         # Secrets (+ External Secrets template)
├── deployments/             # Application deployments
│   ├── backend.yaml         # API server (Bun + Hono)
│   ├── frontend.yaml        # React frontend (nginx)
│   ├── worker.yaml          # BullMQ workers
│   ├── optimizer.yaml       # C++ optimizer with sidecar
│   └── file-watcher.yaml    # Mileage file ingestion
├── services/                # Service definitions
│   ├── backend-service.yaml
│   ├── frontend-service.yaml
│   └── worker-service.yaml
├── ingress/                 # Ingress configuration
│   └── ingress.yaml         # NGINX Ingress + WebSocket
├── hpa/                     # Autoscaling
│   └── backend-hpa.yaml     # HPA + PodDisruptionBudgets
├── network/                 # Network policies
│   └── network-policies.yaml
├── storage/                 # Persistent storage
│   └── pvcs.yaml            # PVCs for file ingestion
└── kustomization.yaml       # Kustomize configuration
```

## Prerequisites

- Kubernetes 1.28+
- NGINX Ingress Controller
- cert-manager (for TLS certificates)
- External Secrets Operator (optional, for production secrets)
- Prometheus + Grafana (for monitoring)

## Deployment

### Development

```bash
# Preview manifests
kubectl kustomize k8s/

# Apply to cluster
kubectl apply -k k8s/
```

### Production

1. Update image tags in `kustomization.yaml`
2. Configure secrets (replace placeholders or use External Secrets)
3. Update ingress hostnames
4. Apply with appropriate context:

```bash
kubectl apply -k k8s/ --context=production
```

## Resource Specifications

| Component | CPU Request | CPU Limit | Memory Request | Memory Limit | Replicas |
|-----------|-------------|-----------|----------------|--------------|----------|
| Backend | 250m | 1000m | 512Mi | 1Gi | 3-10 (HPA) |
| Frontend | 100m | 500m | 128Mi | 256Mi | 2 |
| Worker | 250m | 1000m | 512Mi | 2Gi | 3-10 (HPA) |
| Optimizer Worker | 500m | 2000m | 1Gi | 4Gi | 2 |
| Optimizer Binary | 1000m | 4000m | 2Gi | 8Gi | (sidecar) |
| File Watcher | 100m | 500m | 256Mi | 512Mi | 1 |

## Autoscaling

Backend and Worker deployments use HPA with:
- CPU target: 70% utilization
- Memory target: 80% utilization
- Scale-up: 100% per 15s (aggressive)
- Scale-down: 10% per 60s (conservative)

## Network Policies

Default deny-all policy with explicit allow rules:
- Backend: Ingress from nginx, egress to PostgreSQL/Redis/OTEL
- Frontend: Ingress from nginx only
- Workers: Egress to PostgreSQL/Redis/OTEL, external HTTPS (Maximo)
- All: DNS resolution allowed

## Storage

| PVC | Size | Access Mode | Purpose |
|-----|------|-------------|---------|
| mms-file-inbox | 100Gi | RWX | Incoming mileage files |
| mms-file-archive | 500Gi | RWX | Processed file archive |
| mms-file-errors | 50Gi | RWX | Failed file storage |
| mms-exports | 100Gi | RWX | Generated reports |

## Monitoring

All pods expose Prometheus metrics:
- Backend: `:3000/metrics`
- Workers: `:9090/metrics`
- Optimizer: `:9091/metrics`
- File Watcher: `:9092/metrics`

Annotations enable automatic Prometheus scraping.

## Security

- Non-root containers (UID 1000)
- Read-only root filesystem
- Dropped capabilities
- Seccomp profiles enabled
- Pod Security Standards enforced
- Network policies restrict traffic flow

## Troubleshooting

```bash
# Check pod status
kubectl get pods -n mms

# View logs
kubectl logs -n mms -l app.kubernetes.io/component=backend -f

# Check HPA status
kubectl get hpa -n mms

# Describe deployment issues
kubectl describe deployment mms-backend -n mms
```

## Summary: MMS Monitoring Configuration

### Where Monitoring Config Should Be Placed

**Established location:** `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/mms/system/monitoring/`
- Prometheus config: `monitoring/prometheus/prometheus.yml` (already exists)
- Alert rules: `monitoring/prometheus/alerts.yml` (fully configured)
- AlertManager: `monitoring/alertmanager/alertmanager.yml`
- Grafana dashboards: `monitoring/grafana/dashboards/` (6 JSON dashboards)

**K8s integration:** Add a new `k8s/monitoring/` directory for:
- ConfigMaps for Prometheus/AlertManager config
- Deployment manifests for Prometheus, Grafana, AlertManager stack
- ServiceMonitor CRDs (if using Prometheus Operator)

### What Metrics the App Already Emits

**Prometheus (prom-client)** via `/metrics` endpoint:
- **HTTP:** request count, latency histogram, in-flight requests
- **PM Scheduling:** scheduled count, compliance window days, deferrals
- **Optimizer:** solve duration, job count by status, objective value, fallback usage
- **Fleet:** mileage per train, train status gauge, available trains
- **Drift:** trains requiring replan, mileage percentage drift, analysis count
- **Queues:** job totals (started/completed/failed), job duration, queue depth
- **Ingestion:** files/rows processed, anomalies detected
- **Man-hours:** utilization %, overloaded periods, adjustments
- **Notifications:** queued, sent, digested, batch processing
- **Database:** query duration, connection pool size, error count
- **Maximo:** sync requests, webhooks, polling fallback, circuit breaker, shadow records
- **Rate Limiting:** checks, exceeded events, usage %
- **Costing:** PM cost calculated, TCO projections, variance %

**OpenTelemetry (distributed tracing):** spans with service/operation names, request IDs, user context, error recording

### Most Useful Dashboards (Already Designed)

1. **Fleet Overview** - Train fitness, availability %, grounded/restricted counts
2. **PM Schedule** - Compliance rate, overdue count, depot utilization
3. **Optimizer Performance** - Solve time, success rate, queue backlog, objective trend
4. **API Health** - Request rates, p95 latency, error rates, 5xx distribution
5. **Worker Queue** - Job throughput, failure rate, processing duration
6. **Drift Analysis** - Trains requiring replan, severity distribution, per-train variance

**Alert coverage (18 rules configured):**
- Fleet availability (85%/70% thresholds)
- PM compliance (95% threshold), overdue PMs, depot capacity
- Optimizer failures (10% threshold), queue backlog, slow performance (p95 > 5m)
- API errors (5% threshold), latency (p95 > 1s), downtime
- Worker failures (5%), queue backlog (100 jobs), worker count
- Drift severity (RED count > 5, trains > 10)
- Infrastructure: DB pool exhaustion (90%), Redis errors, memory > 3GB

**Ready for K8s deployment** via ConfigMaps as shown in monitoring/README.md.
